---
title: "BIDS and result IO"
subtitle: "Translate BIDS Stats Models and keep image writes at the boundary."
jupyter: python3
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

The BIDS and IO modules sit at the edges of an analysis: one reads an
external description of a model (a BIDS Stats Model) and turns it into
fmrimod objects you can fit; the other writes fitted results back out as
files. Everything in between is the ordinary modeling workflow — the
file formats stay at the boundary and never leak into the model.

## Translate a Run-level BIDS Stats Model

`translate_run_node` converts a BIDS Stats Model node into a typed model spec,
realized event and baseline models, and semantic contrast artifacts. The model
below is intentionally tiny but uses the same shape as a FitLins-style run
node.

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

import fmrimod as fm
from fmrimod.bids import StatsModelContrast, translate_run_node

stats_model = {
    "Name": "tiny_bids_stats_model",
    "BIDSModelVersion": "1.0.0",
    "Nodes": [{
        "Level": "run",
        "Transformations": [
            {"Name": "Factor", "Input": ["trial_type"]},
            {
                "Name": "Convolve",
                "Input": ["trial_type.face", "trial_type.scene"],
                "Model": "spm",
            },
        ],
        "Model": {
            "X": ["trial_type.face", "trial_type.scene", "framewise_displacement", 1],
        },
        "Contrasts": [{
            "Name": "face_gt_scene",
            "ConditionList": ["trial_type.face", "trial_type.scene"],
            "Weights": [1, -1],
            "Test": "t",
        }],
    }],
}

events = pd.DataFrame({
    "run": 1,
    "onset": [4.0, 12.0, 20.0, 28.0],
    "duration": 1.0,
    "trial_type": ["face", "scene", "face", "scene"],
})
confounds = pd.DataFrame({
    "framewise_displacement": np.linspace(0.0, 1.0, 40),
})
sampling = fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=[40], tr=2.0)

translated = translate_run_node(
    stats_model,
    events=events,
    sampling_frame=sampling,
    confounds=confounds,
)
```

The translation exposes both low-level realized columns and higher-level typed
objects. Use the typed objects for modeling decisions; treat the vectors as
interop artifacts.

```{python}
contrast = translated.contrast_specs["face_gt_scene"]

pd.Series({
    "translation": type(translated).__name__,
    "model_spec": type(translated.model_spec).__name__,
    "contrast": type(contrast).__name__,
    "semantic_contrast": type(contrast.semantic_spec()).__name__,
    "n_columns": len(translated.column_names),
})
```

```{python}
translated.column_names
```

## Writing Results

`write_results(...)` emits a manifest plus BIDS-like stat files. It asks
you to pass an explicit `mask` (and `affine`) when the result cannot
infer them, rather than guessing — the mask defines which voxels the
written maps refer to, so making it explicit keeps the on-disk results
unambiguous.

```python
from fmrimod.io import write_results

manifest = write_results(
    fit,
    "derivatives/fmrimod",
    subject="01",
    task="localizer",
    mask=mask,
    affine=affine,
    overwrite=True,
)
manifest.manifest_path
```

## Next

- [Datasets](datasets.qmd)
- [Group analysis](group-analysis.qmd)
- [Module coverage](module-coverage.qmd)
